name: Schoep — Linear A administration
authors_year: Ilse Schoep, 1999/2002
venue_or_url: AJA 103 (1999) 'Tablets and Territories?'; 'The Administration of Neopalatial Crete', Minos Suppl. (2002)
what_it_does: Human epigraphic/archaeological reconstruction of the Late Minoan IB Linear A administrative apparatus: document typology (tablets, roundels, noduli, nodules), find-context, and inferred political geography of the archives.
notes: Foundational for the CONCEPT of administrative document roles/typology, but entirely by human reading of context; monolingual Linear A; no blinding, no model, no held-out test. Far from the method-novelty; near only in subject.

name: Helena Tomas — Linear A vs Linear B administrative systems
authors_year: Helena Tomas, ~2010–2013 (DPhil Oxford)
venue_or_url: Persée mom_1955-4982_2010 'Linear A versus Linear B administrative systems...'; DPhil 'Understanding the transition between Linear A and Linear B scripts', Oxford
what_it_does: Comparative human study of the three Aegean administrative systems (Cretan Hieroglyphic, Linear A, Linear B): shared clay-tablet accounting vs system-specific documents (roundels/sealings for A; labels/nodules/noduli for B), arguing Minoan practice shaped Mycenaean.
notes: Nearest in this cluster on the SCHEMA axis: directly compares administrative document-families/structures ACROSS A and B. But it is qualitative human epigraphy using full knowledge of Linear B readings — the opposite of blinding — and has no model, no uncertainty quantification, no held-out partition/transfer gate.

name: Davis & Valério — contextual study of HT 85 and HT 117
authors_year: Brent Davis & Miguel Valério, 2020
venue_or_url: NEOTEROS: Studies... in Honor of J.G. Younger (Aegaeum 44), 2020
what_it_does: Contextual analysis of the '1-lists' on Hagia Triada tablets HT 85 and HT 117, testing whether the entries are personal names vs designations of people (i.e., administrative roles), using Linear B anthroponym parallels and cautioning against over-identification.
notes: Nearest on the ROLE axis — 'designations of people' = administrative roles. But it is deliberately UN-blinded: it leans on Linear B phonetic/anthroponym readings to interpret, exactly the phonetic crutch the novelty removes. Single-pair case study, no held-out eval, no model, no calibrated uncertainty.

name: Salgarella — Linear A overview / Aegean Linear Scripts
authors_year: Ester Salgarella, 2020
venue_or_url: Aegean Linear Script(s): Rethinking the Relationship Between Linear A and Linear B, Cambridge Classical Studies, CUP 2020; SigLA database (sigla.phis.me)
what_it_does: Palaeographic/structural study of the genetic relationship between Linear A and Linear B (script vs writing-system distinction, sign-by-sign homomorphy), plus the SigLA palaeographical database (~300 signs / ~400 inscriptions).
notes: Cross-script at the SIGN level (genetic/graphic relationship), not administrative role/schema induction. Human palaeography; SigLA is a data resource (used by logos), not a blinded predictive model. No held-out eval, no uncertainty gate.

name: Salgarella, Bellinato & Ferrara 2025 — spice-related logograms
authors_year: Silvia Ferrara, Ester Salgarella, Laura Bellinato, 2025
venue_or_url: Kadmos 64.1-2 (2025) 29-44, 'On Aegean Spices: Decipherment Prospects on Linear A and B Spice-Related Signs', De Gruyter
what_it_does: Proposes new readings for undeciphered spice/perfumed-oil logograms and monograms on Pylos Linear B tablets by combining contextual analysis with graphic comparison to Linear A and scribal disambiguation strategies.
notes: Explicit DECIPHERMENT/reading goal (semantic values of logograms) — precisely the glossed, un-blinded direction the novelty avoids. Cross-script graphic comparison A<->B, but human, per-sign, no held-out partition, no calibrated uncertainty, no role/schema induction.

name: Corazza et al. — Linear A fraction values
authors_year: Michele Corazza, Barbara Montecchi, Miguel Valério, Fabio Tamburini, Silvia Ferrara, 2021
venue_or_url: Journal of Archaeological Science 125 (2021), 'The mathematical values of fraction signs in the Linear A script: A computational, statistical and typological approach'
what_it_does: Computational+statistical+typological assignment of mathematical values to Linear A fraction signs: enumerates ~4M candidate value-assignments, prunes via constraints and statistical/typological tests, and shows convergence explaining Linear B measurement-sign derivation.
notes: Nearest on METHOD: genuinely computational/statistical with a large hypothesis space and significance-style pruning (uncertainty-aware in a statistical sense), and it connects LA values to LB metrology (cross-script). BUT it targets numeric/metrological values, not administrative ROLE/SCHEMA; it is not blinded (uses cross-script typology openly) and has no sealed held-out/transfer gate. This is th

name: Braović, Krstinić, Štula & Ivanda 2024 — A Systematic Review of Computational Approaches to Deciphering Bronze Age Aegean and Cypriot Scripts
authors_year: Maja Braović, Damir Krstinić, Maja Štula, Antonia Ivanda, 2024
what_it_does: Systematic literature review cataloguing computational decipherment approaches across Archanes, Phaistos Disk, Cretan Hieroglyphic, Linear A, Linear B, Cypro-Minoan and Cypriot scripts. Enumerates 15 challenges in computational decipherment, reviews digital corpora and cross-script links, and outlin
notes: The single most-relevant contextual reference: it maps the entire field including cross-script links and computational challenges, so any reviewer will expect it cited. But it induces nothing and evaluates nothing — cross_script is discussed as a survey topic, not performed. It surveys phonetic/neural-decipherment work; the blinded structure-only role/schema-induction framing does not appear as a

name: SigLA — The Signs of Linear A: A Palæographical Database
authors_year: Ester Salgarella & Simon Castellan, 2020–2021
what_it_does: Open-access palaeographical database: ~300 standard signs, ~400 hand-copied inscriptions, >3,000 individually searchable sign instances with drawings and bounding boxes. Enables statistical and palaeographical analysis of the Linear A epigraphic corpus. Salgarella's associated scholarship argues Lin
notes: A corpus/edition substrate, not an inference method — it is the raw material the novelty programme's blinding operates ON, not a competing method. 'Structure' here means sign-glyph palaeography (shapes/strokes), NOT administrative-role or document-schema induction. Salgarella's A–B homomorphy is human palaeographic comparison, not a learned cross-script transfer gate, so cross_script_transfer=fals

name: DĀMOS — Database of Mycenaean at Oslo
authors_year: Federico Aurora (University of Oslo), 2015 (paper); ongoing
what_it_does: First fully annotated electronic corpus of all published Mycenaean Linear B texts (~5,900 administrative documents). Provides transliterations, phonological transcriptions, scribal-hand attributions, findsite, chronology, logograms, and multi-level annotation that can store competing linguistic inte
notes: The annotated Linear B corpus that the novelty claim HIDES (readings/glosses) — it is the input, not a method. Its multi-level annotation 'accommodates uncertainty' only in the editorial sense of storing rival human interpretations; it is not a probabilistic/calibrated uncertainty-aware model, so uncertainty_aware=false. Carries exactly the phonetic transcriptions/glosses the programme's blinding

name: LiBER — Linear B Electronic Resources
authors_year: Maurizio Del Freo & Francesco Di Filippo (CNR), with Françoise Rougemont, 2013–present (V.2 ~2020)
what_it_does: Integrated digital critical edition of Linear B documents that preserves original scribal LAYOUT — on-screen rendering respects spatial disposition (spacing between signs, gap sizes) plus transcriptions, critical apparatus, photos, findspots, scribes, chronologies. Searchable by document series, syl
notes: Closest in spirit on the 'document-SCHEMA/layout' axis — it deliberately preserves spatial/layout structure of administrative tablets, which is the signal the novelty claim exploits. But LiBER is a human edition/browser: it renders and indexes layout, it does not INDUCE roles or schemas or evaluate on held-out families. No blinding (it displays full Greek readings). A useful layout-feature provena

name: PA-I-TO (pa-i-to) Linear B Epigraphic Project — RTI/3D epigraphy
authors_year: Alessandro Greco & Georgia Flouda, 2017 (ASAtene 95:143–160); with Lavinia Giorgi (graphology/RTI, 2022)
venue_or_url: https://www.paitoproject.it/en/ ; academia.edu/37390708; ASAtene 95 (2017); CREWS Project blog 2022-08-10
what_it_does: Epigraphic/philological project producing a new critical edition of 94 Knossos Linear B tablets mentioning pa-i-to (Phaistos), using non-invasive digital capture: portable 3D laser scanning plus Reflectance Transformation Imaging (RTI) to reproduce engraved sign shape/orientation/stroke sequence as
notes: An imaging/palaeography-and-graphology project operating at the physical-glyph/stroke level (how signs were incised, scribal-hand identification), orthogonal to text-structure role/schema induction. No computational induction, no cross-script transfer, no held-out ML evaluation. Relevant only as a scribal-hand data source; FAR on every axis of the novelty claim.

name: Luo, Cao & Barzilay 2019 — Neural Decipherment via Minimum-Cost Flow: from Ugaritic to Linear B
authors_year: Jiaming Luo, Yuan Cao, Regina Barzilay, 2019
what_it_does: Seq2seq character-level cognate model trained via a minimum-cost-flow objective to recover phonetic correspondences between a lost script and a KNOWN related language (Ugaritic->Hebrew, Linear B->Greek). Reports 67.3% correct cognate translation on Linear B.
notes: The canonical computational-decipherment work in the cluster, but it is fundamentally PHONETIC and REQUIRES a known cognate language — the exact opposite of the blinded, structure-only, gloss-hidden premise. 'Cross-script' here means script->known-language phonetic mapping, not structure-only role transfer. No held-out site/scribe/family partition; evaluated on cognate recovery, not administrative

name: Luo, Hartmann, Santus, Cao & Barzilay 2021 — Deciphering Undersegmented Ancient Scripts Using Phonetic Prior
authors_year: Jiaming Luo, Frederik Hartmann, Enrico Santus, Yuan Cao, Regina Barzilay, 2021
what_it_does: Joint segmentation + phonetic decipherment of undersegmented scripts using a phonetic/linguistic prior and neural sequence modelling; tested on scripts like Gothic and Ugaritic.
notes: Same Barzilay lineage; its central mechanism is a PHONETIC prior — precisely what the novelty claim excludes and blinds against. No administrative role/schema, no held-out site/scribe eval, no uncertainty calibration. In-cluster but methodologically orthogonal.

name: Papavassileiou, Kokkinos & Alexandridis 2023 — A Generative Model for the Mycenaean Linear B Script (text infilling)
authors_year: Katerina Papavassileiou, Dimitrios I. Kosmopoulos, et al., 2023
what_it_does: Bidirectional RNN generative language model of Mycenaean Greek in Linear B that infills/restores damaged words on tablets; beats n-gram baselines. Authors state an aspiration to later apply Linear-B-trained models to Linear A.
notes: This is the 'Papavassileiou Linear B restoration' work named in the brief. It USES the actual Greek readings (fully un-blinded), does character/word restoration not administrative role/schema induction, and does not transfer cross-script (transfer is only future aspiration). Held-out masked-token eval exists but on word-forms with known glosses, not blinded roles.

name: Papavassileiou et al. 2024 — Restoring Mycenaean Linear B 'A&B' series tablets
authors_year: Papavassileiou et al., 2024
what_it_does: Follow-up restoration/infilling of specific Knossos Linear B tablet series using neural language models.
notes: Restoration task on read Linear B; readings fully available. Not role/schema induction, not blinded, not cross-script.

name: Neural Representation Learning for Scribal Hands of Linear B
what_it_does: Self-supervised/representation learning over Linear B sign images to characterize and cluster scribal hands (paleography), enabling held-out scribe analysis.
notes: Genuinely structure/representation-only and does held-out-scribe generalization — overlaps the 'held-out scribe' evaluation axis — but the TASK is paleographic scribal-hand identity, not administrative role/document-schema induction, and there is no cross-script transfer or reading-blinding ablation.

name: Born, Monroe, Kelley & Sarkar 2025 — Disambiguating Numeral Sequences to Decipher Ancient Accounting Corpora
authors_year: Logan Born, M. Willis Monroe, Kathryn Kelley, Anoop Sarkar, 2025
what_it_does: Disambiguates multi-valued proto-Elamite numeral signs using structural properties of administrative documents plus bootstrapped classifiers with a 'cautious rule selection' (uncertainty-aware) mechanism; contributes a held-out test set.
notes: The single nearest computational method: it operates STRUCTURE-ONLY on an UNDECIPHERED administrative/accounting corpus (readings genuinely unavailable), is explicitly UNCERTAINTY-AWARE (cautious rule selection), and ships a held-out test set. But it is numeral-value disambiguation, not role/schema induction over document families; there is NO cross-script transfer gate, NO deliberate blinding-as-

name: Born, Kelley, Kambhatla, Chen & Sarkar 2019 — Sign Clustering and Topic Extraction in Proto-Elamite
authors_year: Logan Born, Kate Kelley, Nishant Kambhatla, Carolyn Chen, Anoop Sarkar, 2019
what_it_does: Unsupervised hierarchical clustering, n-gram statistics, and LDA topic models over the undeciphered proto-Elamite administrative corpus; replicates some manual findings and surfaces novel sign relationships as a decipherment aid.
notes: Structure-only, distributional induction of sign groupings/'topics' on an undeciphered accounting corpus — conceptually adjacent to schema induction — but it is exploratory unsupervised clustering with NO held-out generalization test, NO uncertainty gate, and NO cross-script transfer. Predecessor of the 2025 paper.

name: Contextual reconstruction of Linear A administrative meaning without decipherment (2026 scholarship)
authors_year: unattributed survey/essay coverage, 2026 (GreekReporter Jan 2026; gertitashkomd overview)
what_it_does: Argues that much of the economic/administrative CONTENT of Linear A tablets is recoverable by human deductive analysis of administrative logic, numeral structure, and archaeological context WITHOUT reading the Minoan language.
notes: The closest CONCEPTUAL statement of 'recover administrative roles without decipherment/glosses,' so worth citing to frame the idea — but it is a human, non-computational, non-benchmarked scholarly argument: no model, no blinding ablation, no cross-script transfer gate, no held-out partition, no preregistration/uncertainty. It motivates the claim rather than pre-empting the method.

name: Machine Learning for Ancient Languages: A Survey (Sommerschield et al. 2023)
authors_year: Thea Sommerschield, Yannis Assael, et al., 2023
what_it_does: Field-wide survey of ML for ancient languages (restoration, attribution, decipherment, OCR/HTR).
notes: Used as a coverage check: the survey's decipherment coverage is dominated by phonetic/restoration/attribution work (Luo/Barzilay, Ithaca/Aeneas, restoration LMs). No entry describes a blinded, structure-only administrative role/schema benchmark with a cross-script transfer gate — consistent with (though not proof of) novelty. Absence in a survey is not proof; treat as corroborating, not decisive.
